title: Cream Cheese Cookie Bars
ingredients: 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons butter, room temperature
instructions: Cream the butter and cream cheese together until smooth and well blended.
Add eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
Gradually add cake flour, folding in completely.
Pour batter into a lightly greased 10" x 15" jelly roll pan.
For frosting, cream butter and cream cheese together until well combined.
Add remaining powdered sugar and beat until smooth.
Add additional cream or sugar if needed to reach spreading consistency.
Spread frosting on top of cooled bars.
Refrigerate until frosting sets or place in freezer for 10 minutes.
Cut into bars, wiping knife after each slice.
notes: For mini cakes variation: Cut baked batter into small circular cakes with a 1-1½ inch biscuit cutter to make about 80 mini cakes. Tint frosting desired color, pipe onto cakes using an icing bag with large star tip, and decorate with pearl, silver dragees, or other sprinkles. For a Tiffany Blue tint reminiscent of Katie and Patrick's wedding reception.

For chocolate inspired variation: Add 1½ cups mini chocolate chips to batter after folding in vanilla. After frosting cooled bars, freeze 5 minutes, then drizzle with chocolate glaze in a web pattern and freeze 5 minutes to set. Keep at room temperature or refrigerated.
Chocolate glaze: Melt ⅔ cup Ghiradelli Double Chocolate chips (or regular semisweet chips) with 4 tablespoons butter in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly.

For raspberry lemon bars variation: Add zest of 1 lemon and 1 teaspoon fresh lemon juice to batter at the same time as vanilla. After placing batter in pan, sprinkle with 2-3 pints fresh washed and dried raspberries and press lightly into batter. Bake 5-10 minutes longer. For frosting, use only 1 tablespoon whipping cream and substitute fresh lemon juice for the remaining liquid. Top each cooled square with a fresh raspberry.
